response:
The heavyweight boxer is famous for his incredible power punches.
His aggressive fighting style has thrilled fans around the world.
He faced a professional opponent in a series of three matches.
The first match was a highly anticipated event for everyone.
In that initial bout, he landed exactly 120 power punches.
This number set a high standard for his performance.
The second match brought even more excitement to the arena.
During this second fight, he increased his power punch count.
He improved his total by a significant twenty-five percent.
Calculating this increase requires some basic mathematical steps.
Twenty-five percent of one hundred twenty is thirty punches.
Adding thirty to the original one hundred twenty gives one hundred fifty.
So, in the second match, he landed one hundred fifty power punches.
The third match was the final chapter of this trilogy.
He continued to dominate the ring with his powerful strikes.
In this third encounter, he landed thirty more than the second match.
One hundred fifty plus thirty equals one hundred eighty punches.
This means he landed one hundred eighty power punches in the third fight.
Now we must sum the totals from all three separate matches.
The first match contributed one hundred twenty to the grand total.
The second match added one hundred fifty to the running sum.
The third match provided one hundred eighty additional punches.
Adding one hundred twenty and one hundred fifty gives two hundred seventy.
Then we add the one hundred eighty from the third match to that sum.
Two hundred seventy plus one hundred eighty equals four hundred fifty.
Therefore, the total number of power punches is four hundred fifty.
This impressive total showcases his relentless attacking style.
The crowd was likely in awe of such a high number of strikes.
His ability to maintain power across three fights is remarkable.
Each match showed a clear progression in his offensive output.
The first match was strong, but the second was stronger still.
The third match proved he could sustain his intensity even longer.
Four hundred fifty power punches is a truly legendary statistic.
